source.title: Paul's Relationship with the Jerusalem Apostles
source.summary: Paul's letters are the earliest New Testament documents (c. 48-62 AD), written within 15-30 years of Jesus' crucifixion. He met with the original apostles in Jerusalem (Galatians 1-2), and his core teachings about Jesus' death, resurrection, and divine status align with the Jerusalem church's beliefs. 1 Corinthians 15:3-8 contains a creed Paul 'received' from earlier Christians (c. 30s AD), showing continuity with pre-Pauline Christian belief. Historical scholarship recognizes Paul as faithfully transmitting rather than inventing core Christian doctrines.
